name: Aspire
description: Singapore B2B neobank and finance OS for SEA SMBs — multi-currency accounts, corporate cards, payouts and expense APIs.

seo.title: Aspire — Payments Orchestration
seo.meta_description: Aspire: Singapore B2B neobank and finance OS for SEA SMBs — multi-currency accounts, corporate cards, payouts and expense APIs.

when_to_use: Multi-currency business accounts for SEA startups and SMBs
Corporate card + expense management for distributed SEA teams
API-driven payouts to vendors and employees across Southeast Asia
Embedded finance APIs for B2B platforms serving SEA merchants

pros: Purpose-built for SEA SMBs with local compliance baked in
Multi-currency accounts, cards, and payouts in one stack
Expense and AP automation reduces finance tooling sprawl
Modern API and dashboard vs. legacy SEA banks
cons: Coverage outside Southeast Asia is limited
Not a true orchestrator for third-party PSPs
Card program features weaker than US neobanks
Support SLA varies by tier and market
review.verdict: Strong SEA-first finance OS for SMBs, but miscategorised as orchestration — it is really a neobank with APIs.
review.best_for: Singapore, Indonesia, Vietnam SMBs wanting a unified account, card, and payout platform.
review.not_for: Merchants needing multi-PSP smart routing or global coverage beyond SEA.

lock_in.reason: Neobank, money portable but integrations sticky
